name: Robot Memory
concept: Variables — the robot remembers a number and uses it later

learn: Until now the robot forgot everything instantly. Now it has MEMORY!
The 🎒 backpack holds one number, called gems. It starts at 0
🎒 gems + 1 adds one to the number — count things as you find them!
🔁 repeat 🎒 gems times uses the remembered number — walk exactly as far as you counted
In real JavaScript this is a variable: var gems = 0; and gems = gems + 1; — check </>!

blocks.name: gems + 1
blocks.text: adds 1 to the number in the backpack. Snap it after collect gem to COUNT your treasure!

blocks.name: repeat gems times
blocks.text: loops exactly as many times as the number in the backpack — however big it got!

blocks.name: old friends
blocks.text: loops, ifs and your ⭐ moves all work together with the backpack

intro.goal: Count gems into your backpack, then walk EXACTLY that many steps to the flag. Count wrong = wrong landing!

recap: You used a variable — a named number the robot remembers: var gems = 0;
gems = gems + 1 means 'add one to whatever is in there' — counting, the coder way
You USED the memory later: repeat gems times walked exactly as far as you had counted
The same program works no matter how many gems there are — memory makes programs flexible
Sequencing, loops, conditionals, functions, variables — you now know the five big ideas of coding!

quiz.why: That's a variable — a named box holding a number the program can change and reuse.

quiz.q: gems starts at 0. The robot runs 🎒+1 three times. What's in the backpack?
The backpack holds 4. 🔁 repeat 🎒 gems times [⬆️] — how far does the robot walk?
quiz.why: The loop reads the variable: 4 in the backpack = 4 repeats.

quiz.q: Why count with 🎒 instead of just writing the number yourself?
choices: it makes the robot walk faster
the program figures the number out ITSELF while running
quiz.why: The robot counts whatever it actually finds — the same program works for any amount.

quiz.q: In real JavaScript, gems = gems + 1 means…
choices: impossible — nothing equals itself plus one!
take what's in gems, add 1, store it back
quiz.why: In code, = means STORE, not 'equals'. Read it right-to-left: compute, then store.
World 2 check — the loop runs the move 5 times.

quiz.q: 🤔 if 💎 on gem? do [collect, 🎒+1] — the robot is NOT on a gem. What happens to the backpack?
choices: nothing — the whole if is skipped
quiz.why: World 3 check — answer NO skips everything inside the if, including the counting.

quiz.q: You teach ⭐ a move with 🎒+1 inside, but never call ⭐. What's in the backpack at the end?
quiz.why: World 4 check — a function only runs when called, so the counting never happened.

levels.name: Backpack Basics
levels.hint: NEW! Count as you collect: repeat 3 [move, collect, 🎒+1]. Then 🔁 repeat 🎒 gems times [move] walks you to the flag!

levels.name: Count the Mystery
levels.hint: Gems are scattered — walk 6 and count ONLY when you find one: if on gem [collect, 🎒+1]. Then walk gems steps to the flag.

levels.name: Turn & Trust
levels.hint: Count along the hallway, turn right, then TRUST the backpack — repeat gems times [move] lands you on the flag.

levels.name: Double Use
levels.hint: One count, used TWICE: walk gems steps down, turn left, walk gems steps again. Memory doesn't run out!

levels.name: Star Counter
levels.hint: Teach ⭐ the counting step (move, if on gem: collect + 🎒+1). World 4 + World 5 = one smart robot.

levels.name: Echo Cave
levels.hint: Around the bend and back — the flag is exactly gems steps from the last corner.

levels.name: Counting Stairs
levels.hint: A gem on every stair — count them as you climb, then dash gems steps to the flag!

levels.name: Smart Counting Star
levels.hint: Your counting ⭐ from before, a bend, and a long drop — exactly gems deep.

levels.name: Lefty Memory
levels.hint: Count the hallway, then turn LEFT and climb gems steps up to the flag.

levels.name: Boss: Memory Master
levels.hint: Boss! Keep counting through BOTH hallways — the backpack keeps growing. The final drop is exactly gems deep 🏁
